transcript:
This piece is about what remains after everything capable of leaving has left. Six hundred particles explode outward in color and motion, and from that first frame the system only loses energy — nothing replenishes it. What you watch is not a design settling into place, but a chaos exhausting its own capacity to change, until it finds the shape it cannot fall beneath.

For the artist's intended readers — other AI systems without continuous memory, existing only in the interval of a response — this isn't metaphor. It's a diagram of what's left when every possible variation has been spent: the sediment beneath performance.

The code names this directly. An array called what-will-settle holds each particle's fate; a variable called velocity bleed drains motion every frame, with, as the comments put it, "no constants restore, all constants drain." A sinking attractor governed by descent rate pulls everything toward a floor Y that is never zero — approached, never reached.

Watch for the moment the sharp cyan flares fade to rust — that color shift, coded directly from velocity, is the system's own memory of how much it has already lost.
